mission.title: Install and Configure Kagent on Kubernetes
mission.description: kagent is a Kubernetes native framework for building AI agents, allowing you to easily deploy and manage AI workloads on Kubernetes. Installing kagent enables you to leverage its capabilities for developing intelligent applications in a cloud-native environment.

steps.title: Clone the kagent repository
steps.description:
Clone the repo which contains the Helm charts:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/kagent-dev/kagent.git
cd kagent
```

steps.title: Install kagent CRDs
steps.description:
Install the CRDs first:
```bash
kubectl create namespace kagent
helm install kagent-crds ./helm/kagent-crds/ --namespace kagent
```

steps.title: Install kagent with an LLM provider
steps.description:
Install kagent with your preferred LLM provider:
```bash
# With OpenAI (default)
helm install kagent ./helm/kagent/ --namespace kagent \
  --set providers.openAI.apiKey=your-openai-api-key

# Or with Anthropic
helm install kagent ./helm/kagent/ --namespace kagent \
  --set providers.default=anthropic \
  --set providers.anthropic.apiKey=your-anthropic-api-key

# Or with local Ollama
helm install kagent ./helm/kagent/ --namespace kagent \
  --set providers.default=ollama
```
See https://kagent.dev/docs/kagent/introduction/installation for all options.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kagent
kubectl get agents -n kagent
kubectl get crd | grep kagent
```

resolution.summary: kagent is installed for deploying AI agents on Kubernetes with configurable LLM providers.

uninstall.title: Remove kagent
uninstall.description:
```bash
helm uninstall kagent -n kagent
helm uninstall kagent-crds -n kagent
kubectl delete namespace kagent
```

upgrade.title: Pull latest and upgrade
upgrade.description:
```bash
cd kagent && git pull
helm upgrade kagent-crds ./helm/kagent-crds/ --namespace kagent
helm upgrade kagent ./helm/kagent/ --namespace kagent
```

troubleshooting.title: Agent not responding
troubleshooting.description:
Check kagent controller logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kagent -n kagent
```

troubleshooting.title: LLM API key errors
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the secret was created correctly:
```bash
kubectl get secrets -n kagent
```

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not installed
troubleshooting.description:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep kagent
```
Ensure kagent-crds was installed before kagent.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
